ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] [Scouting for Wild Ones](https://agentlefeast.com/product/scoutingbook/) by Brittney McGann is full of great lessons and activities for your budding scout.
[ ] Choose a museum from [this list](https://studenttravelplanningguide.com/virtual-field-trips/) of museums by state and take a virtual field trip. (A parent should preview all links.)
[ ] Try a recipe from [one of the states](https://a.co/d/6QqQ4JL) you are studying. Check your library or online for ideas.
[ ] Enjoy the book [Climbing Kansas Mountains by George Shannon](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RsnkhOwkvSA), read aloud on YouTube.

[ ] Kentucky has more than 130 caves and caverns. Read about seven remarkable ones in this [Smithsonian article](https://www.smithsonianmag.com/sponsored/seven-remarkable-caves-explore-kentucky-180976889/).
[ ] Visit this amazing[ 3-dimensional map ](https://lh3.ggpht.com/-2FH77Y-374M/UZymFOTGbkI/AAAAAAAAor0/jQSL7DT1hTY/mud-island-riverwalk-2%25255B8%25255D.jpg?imgmax=800)of the lower Mississippi River if you are near Memphis. It is on Mud Island at Riverpark.
[ ] Learn about the Jr. Ranger Program at this [nps site](https://www.nps.gov/kids/junior-rangers.htm). There are activities you can even do from home.

[ ] Take a [virtual tour](https://www.nationalparks.org/connect/blog/take-virtual-visit-national-park) or watch a webcam from a National Park.
[ ] Take a [virtual field trip](https://youtu.be/F7K2Vmog8nk?si=asQoMwFrQzttgjxp) to Washington, D.C.
[ ] You can find out what indigenous groups lived in your zip code by entering it [here](https://land.codeforanchorage.org/).
